title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
coreIdea: Find the maximum sum path between any two nodes in a binary tree, where the path need not pass through the root. At each node, compute the max single-side contribution upward, while separately tracking the best 'through-node' path as a global maximum.
recognitionCues: asks for max path sum, not restricted to root-to-leaf
tree contains negative values, requiring care about whether to include a branch
path can start and end anywhere
approach: Define a recursive function returning the max single-path sum extending from a node downward (ignoring negative contributions by taking max(0, subtree_sum)). At each node, update a global max using node.val + left_contribution + right_contribution (the 'through node' path), but return only node.val + max(left, right) upward to the parent.
